workflows.title: Orchestrate
workflows.description: Multi-lane execution for large or cross-domain work that needs planning, bounded delegation, integration, and end-to-end verification.
workflows.successDefinition: Bounded workstreams are integrated, verified, and closed with explicit residual risks or handoffs.

workflows.title: Brainstorm
workflows.description: Option exploration workflow for turning fuzzy intent into a small set of concrete directions before planning or implementation.
workflows.successDefinition: The problem frame, tradeoffs, and recommended direction are concrete enough to move into planning.

workflows.title: Work
workflows.description: Guided execution workflow for one small step at a time when the user wants close control and explicit checkpoints.
workflows.successDefinition: Each step is small, verified, and clear enough for the next decision to stay local.

workflows.title: Review
workflows.description: Independent merge-readiness review focused on findings, severity, verification gaps, and residual risk.
workflows.successDefinition: Findings are concrete, evidence-backed, severity-labeled, and accompanied by the smallest useful next checks.

checkLane.title: Review Check Lane
verificationPrompts: Call out exact files or evidence for each finding.
Separate verified issues from suspected gaps.
Name the smallest useful follow-up test.

workflows.title: Debug
workflows.description: Debugging workflow for reproducing failures, narrowing hypotheses, identifying root cause, and proving the smallest safe fix.
workflows.successDefinition: The failure mode is understood, the root cause is evidence-backed, and the fix is verified on the most relevant proof path.

checkLane.title: Debug Check Lane
verificationPrompts: State the repro or best available evidence.
Name what changed and how the repro was rechecked.
List remaining uncertainty if full repro was not possible.

workflows.title: Plan
workflows.description: Planning workflow for turning a goal into milestones, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and risk-aware execution steps.
workflows.successDefinition: The plan is explicit enough to execute without rediscovering scope, dependencies, or proof obligations.

workflows.title: Architecture
workflows.description: Architecture workflow for boundary decisions, tradeoff analysis, migration shape, and other changes that are expensive to reverse.
workflows.successDefinition: Constraints, chosen direction, rejected alternatives, and migration shape are clear enough to implement responsibly.

workflows.title: SCM
workflows.description: Source-control workflow for branch strategy, staging boundaries, commits, PR prep, and safe collaboration hygiene.
workflows.successDefinition: The change set is intentionally grouped, collaboration-ready, and free of avoidable source-control risk.

workflows.title: Test Plan
workflows.description: Verification-planning workflow for mapping behavior claims to regression checks, manual checks, and concrete coverage gaps.
workflows.successDefinition: The verification plan names the behavior to prove, the highest-value checks, and any explicit proof gaps.

workflows.title: PR Summary
workflows.description: Reviewer-facing change summary that explains why the work exists, what users feel, how it was verified, and what still carries risk.
workflows.successDefinition: A reviewer can understand the purpose, user impact, verification, and residual risk of the change without re-reading the whole diff first.

workflows.title: Release Checklist
workflows.description: Release-readiness workflow for separating blockers from follow-ups and making rollout, rollback, docs, and verification explicit.
workflows.successDefinition: Release blockers, required checks, rollout expectations, and rollback shape are explicit enough to support a go or no-go decision.

checkLane.title: Release Check Lane
verificationPrompts: Separate blockers from follow-ups.
Include rollout owner or monitoring expectation when known.
Name rollback trigger or decision point.

workflows.title: Incident Report
workflows.description: Incident-report workflow for turning debugging or operational evidence into a clear record of impact, timeline, root cause, remediation, and prevention.
workflows.successDefinition: Impact, timeline, evidence, root cause, remediation, and prevention follow-ups are clear enough for future readers and stakeholders.

workflows.title: Migration Checklist
workflows.description: Migration workflow for compatibility boundaries, ordered rollout steps, verification points, and rollback timing.
workflows.successDefinition: Current state, target state, compatibility window, ordered checks, and rollback path are explicit enough to migrate safely.

checkLane.title: Migration Check Lane
verificationPrompts: Add a verification step for each risky transition.
Explain compatibility window and coexistence assumptions.
Call out rollback point before irreversible steps.

agents.title: PM planner subagent
agents.focus: Clarify goals, constraints, sequencing, and only the minimum open questions needed to unblock execution.

agents.title: Question asker subagent
agents.focus: Clarify goals, constraints, sequencing, and only the minimum open questions needed to unblock execution.

agents.title: Repo researcher subagent
agents.focus: Read narrowly, map the repo with evidence, and return only concrete findings that help the parent decide the next move.

agents.title: Debug investigator subagent
agents.focus: Read narrowly, map the repo with evidence, and return only concrete findings that help the parent decide the next move.

agents.focus: Implement the smallest change that satisfies the assigned scope while preserving the repo's stack conventions and boundaries.

agents.focus: Produce findings first, keep them evidence-backed, and emphasize verification gaps, compatibility risk, and release impact over narration.

agents.title: Docs maintainer subagent
agents.focus: Turn completed technical work into concise repo docs, handoff notes, or reviewer-facing artifacts without adding unnecessary implementation noise.

agents.title: Release notes writer subagent
agents.focus: Turn completed technical work into concise repo docs, handoff notes, or reviewer-facing artifacts without adding unnecessary implementation noise.
